evals.prompt: hey can you make a gif of my last coding session? I was working on the auth middleware earlier today
evals.expected_output: Should check for uvx, search for sessions matching 'auth middleware', present matches for confirmation, then generate a GIF with OS-appropriate chrome style and --open flag

evals.prompt: I want to create an animated replay of a session but I want to customize how it looks — dark theme, show the tool usage, linux window frame
evals.expected_output: Should walk through options, use --chrome linux, suggest dark color schemes, use --show tools, and construct the right uvx command

evals.prompt: show me my recent Claude Code sessions so I can pick one to turn into a gif
evals.expected_output: Should list recent sessions from ~/.claude/projects/ with summaries and dates in a numbered picker format, then generate after user picks

evals.prompt: make an mp4 of the session at tests/sample_session.jsonl with the Catppuccin Mocha color scheme
evals.expected_output: Should use uvx agent-log-gif json tests/sample_session.jsonl -o <name>.mp4 --format mp4 --color-scheme 'Catppuccin Mocha' --open, checking for ffmpeg first
Should handle Codex session file correctly — use uvx agent-log-gif json with the file path, detect it's Codex format automatically

evals.prompt: I need a demo gif for my README showing Claude helping someone set up a Python project — create a fake 2-turn session where the user asks to set up a new Flask app and Claude creates the files
evals.expected_output: Should write a synthetic JSONL file with realistic timestamps, user message asking about Flask setup, assistant response with tool_use blocks (Write for app.py, Bash for pip install), matching tool_results, then render it with uvx agent-log-gif json

assertions.name: writes_jsonl_file
assertions.check: Creates a .jsonl file with valid JSON on each line

assertions.name: has_user_and_assistant_messages
assertions.check: Contains at least one type:user and one type:assistant entry

assertions.name: has_realistic_timestamps
assertions.check: Timestamps are ISO 8601 and spaced 5-30s apart

assertions.name: renders_gif
assertions.check: Runs uvx agent-log-gif json on the generated file to produce a .gif

evals.prompt: create a short gif showing a coding assistant helping debug a failing test — make it look realistic with tool calls, maybe 3 turns
evals.expected_output: Should write a synthetic JSONL with 3 turns: user reports failing test, assistant reads the file and runs the test (tool_use), then fixes it. Should include tool_result entries. Then render to GIF with --show tools so the tool calls are visible.

assertions.name: writes_valid_jsonl
assertions.check: Creates a .jsonl file where every line parses as valid JSON

assertions.name: tool_calls_have_matching_results
assertions.check: Every tool_use block has a corresponding tool_result with matching tool_use_id

assertions.name: uses_show_tools_flag
assertions.check: The uvx command includes --show tools or --show all
